description: §65.3.2 / §65.4.1 / §65.10 — a flat-declaration `#{}` (a component-root child) references `@nope`, which matches neither an in-scope `<theme>` token nor a declared reactive cell. The flat-inline `#{}`→`style=""` path runs the IDENTICAL decidable use-site check as the scoped-selector path and fires E-THEME-TOKEN-UNKNOWN — so the theme feature's error semantics hold in the dominant `#{}` pattern, not only inside a selector block.
rationale: §65.10: E-THEME-TOKEN-UNKNOWN is the decidable `@`-sigil use-site check; a bare identifier is literal CSS and never fires. The flat-inline path reuses lowerCssValueRefs, so the same membership + error semantics apply — this proves the neg case fires in the flat `#{}`→style= emission path. Partitions into result.errors (E- prefix).
